response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and systems developer with notable GIS platform experience, but the role demands deep, production-grade modern C++ (or Rust) in routing or navigation data pipelines — a requirement he does not meet. His C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project; his primary commercial stack across the last decade is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. While his GISCollective work demonstrates relevant geospatial thinking and his Sociomantic role shows he can reason about high-throughput distributed systems, neither substitutes for the core C++ expertise and routing/navigation domain knowledge this role requires.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial platform development via GISCollective, including event-driven geo-data pipelines for real-world NGO use cases
Demonstrated distributed-systems experience at Sociomantic — building low-latency micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O and high-performance data stores
Broad multi-language and multi-platform background (DLang, C#, Java, Node.js, Swift) signals adaptability and systems-level thinking beyond web scripting
concerns: No substantive modern C++ or Rust experience in production environments — the role's primary technical requirement is unmet
Zero domain experience in routing tiles, navigation pipelines, or traffic-signal integration; GIS work is map-data collection, not routing optimization
Career trajectory is strongly web/TypeScript-centric; re-skilling to production C++ for a latency-sensitive geospatial pipeline would represent a major and risky transition
